    "text": "(a)\nTo represent the solution of a linear inequality in one or two variables graphically\nin a plane, we proceed as follows:\n(i)\nIf the inequality involves ‘≥’ or ‘≤’, we draw the graph of the line as a thick\nline to indicate that the points on this line are included in the solution set.\n(ii)\nIf the inequality involves ‘>’ or ‘<’, we draw the graph of the line as dotted\nline to indicate that the points on the line are excluded from the solution set.\n(b)\nSolution of a linear inequality in one variable can be represented on number line\nas well as in the plane but the solution of a linear inequality in two variables of the\ntype ax + by > c, ax + by ≥ c, ax + by < c or ax + by ≤ c (a ≠ 0, b ≠ 0) can be\nrepresented in the plane only.\n(c)\nTwo or more inequalities taken together comprise a system of inequalities and\nthe solutions of the system of inequalities are the solutions common to all the\ninequalities comprising the system.",

How many items must be sold to realise some profit?\nSolution  We have,\nprofit = Revenue – Cost\n= (60x + 2000) – (20x + 4000)\n= 40x – 2000\nTo earn some profit, 40x – 2000 > 0\n⇒\nx > 50\nHence, the manufacturer must sell more than 50 items to realise some profit.",

Therefore, we\nmust have 2x + 3y ≥ 3 as linear\ninequality corresponding to the line\n2x + 3y = 3.\n(ii)\nConsider 3x + 4y = 18. We observe\nthat the shaded region and the origin\nlie on the same side of this line and\n(0, 0) satisfies 3x + 4y ≤ 18. Therefore,\n3x + 4y ≤ 18 is the linear inequality\ncorresponding to the line 3x + 4y = 18.\n(iii)\nConsider –7x + 4y = 14. It is clear from the figure that the shaded region\nand the origin lie on the same side of this line and (0, 0) satisfies the inequality\n– 7x + 4y ≤ 14. Therefore, –7x + 4y ≤ 14 is the inequality corresponding to the\nline –7x + 4y = 14.\n(iv)\nConsider x – 6y = 3. It may be noted that the shaded portion and origin lie on the\nsame side of this line and (0, 0) satisfies x – 6y ≤ 3. Therefore, x – 6y ≤ 3 is the\ninequality corresponding to the line x – 6y = 3.\n(v)\nAlso the shaded region lies in the first quadrant only. Therefore, x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0.\nHence, in view of (i), (ii), (iii), (iv) and (v) above, the linear inequalities\ncorresponding to the given solution set are :\n2x + 3y ≥ 3,3x + 4y ≤ 18 –7x + 4y ≤14, x – 6y ≤ 3, x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0.",
